behavior_summary: When the SKULTOSS instance 'hatches' it searches the nearby area for world items and forcibly throws eligible items away from the U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch point.
A vertical threshold is computed from the U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch's Z coordinate (base_z = Item::getZ(U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch) - 0x20); only items with Z > base_z are considered for tossing.

For each candidate in the search area the script obtains the candidate Z, filters by the threshold, generates randomized components (via urandom and a helper FREE::1A96), and calls Item::hurl on the candidate to impart randomized velocity/offsets.
This produces an immediate outward-toss animation/physics effect on nearby items when the skull-like creature/item hatches.
notable_control_flow: Entry on the 'U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch' event triggers immediate execution (no long-lived state retained).
Compute base_z from the U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch actor's Z, then run an item-iterator over a radius derived from a constant multiplication.
Per-item branch: if candidate.Z <= base_z then skip; otherwise compute random offsets/components and call Item::hurl(candidate, ...).
After hurling each qualifying item the loop continues until the search iterator is exhausted, then the handler returns.
open_questions: Exact numeric interpretation of the search radius (derived from a constant multiplication in the disassembly) and which item types/shapes, if any, are filtered by the iterator tokens.
Precise semantics and mapping of FREE::1A96 (what kind of signed/normalized component it returns) and how its inputs map to hurl's intended velocity/angle axes.
Exact parameter ordering and units expected by Item::hurl(...) (x/y/z, force/angle mapping is inferred but not explicit).
Whether some categories of items (e.g., anchored/world-fixed objects) are implicitly excluded by the iterator or by Item::hurl failure modes.

main_function.name: U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch
main_function.purpose: On U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch, scan the immediate area and forcibly throw nearby items that are above a computed vertical threshold to create a randomized toss effect.
entry_conditions: Event 7 (U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch) — called when the SKULTOSS instance hatches or triggers its U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch
function_map.role: Event handler that implements the toss-on-U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch behavior for a SKULTOSS object.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Engine API to read an item's vertical (Z) coordinate; used to compute threshold and inspect candidates.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random-number provider used to add variability to toss parameters.

function_map.name: FREE::1A96
function_map.role: Helper routine that transforms inputs into a signed/normalized toss component (inferred).

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Engine API that applies a throw/hurl effect to an item instance (imparts motion/offsets).

external_calls.purpose: Read the U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch actor Z to compute base_z and read each candidate's Z to decide eligibility for tossing.
Generate small random values used to vary the hurl parameters (adds unpredictability to push strength/direction).
Compute/convert bytes/inputs into a signed component used as one or more hurl parameters (inferred mapping to velocity/angle components).
Apply the computed/randomized throw to the qualifying candidate item so it visibly/tactically gets tossed from the U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch point.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the SKULTOSS instance 'hatches' it searches the nearby area for world items and forcibly throws eligible items away from the U8P_SKULTOSS::hatch point.
